lines: "The joy of the Lord is your strength." Nehemiah 8:10.
Not your coffee. [short pause] Not your willpower. [short pause] Not your to-do list. [short pause] The joy of the Lord. [short pause] That's your strength today.

Quick, sharp breath in through your nose... [short pause] [short pause] short exhale. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] out. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] out. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] out. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] out. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] out. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] out.
Now a slow, deep breath in through your nose... [short pause] [short pause] fill your whole chest... [short pause] [short pause] and let it all go.

Good. [short pause] You can feel the shift. [short pause] Your blood is moving. [short pause] Your lungs are open. [short pause] Your body is waking up with you. [short pause] That change you feel... [short pause] [short pause] that alertness... [short pause] [short pause] it happened in seconds. [short pause] One breathing pattern and your whole physiology responded. [short pause] Remember that. [short pause] You are not stuck in whatever state you wake up in.

Stretch your arms overhead. [short pause] Reach as high as you can. [short pause] Feel your spine lengthen, your ribcage expand. [short pause] Hold that stretch for a moment... [short pause] [short pause] reaching... [short pause] [short pause] reaching... [short pause] [short pause] and release.

Now roll your shoulders back... [short pause] [short pause] one... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three times. [short pause] And let them settle down and back. [short pause] Feel the openness across your chest. [short pause] That's the posture of someone who isn't bracing for the day... [short pause] [short pause] but stepping into it.

Unclench your jaw. [short pause] Soften your face. [short pause] Let your tongue drop from the roof of your mouth. [short pause] Relax the muscles around your eyes. [short pause] So much tension lives in the face without us knowing it.

Now press your feet into the floor. [short pause] Feel the ground under you. [short pause] Solid. [short pause] Stable. [short pause] Push down slightly and feel the floor push back. [short pause] You're here. [short pause] You're awake. [short pause] And you're awake for a reason.

The context of this verse matters. [short pause] Nehemiah's people had just heard the Law of God read aloud for the first time in generations. [short pause] They were weeping... [short pause] [short pause] overwhelmed by how far they'd fallen, how much they'd lost, how long they'd been disconnected from who they were meant to be. [short pause] And Nehemiah said... [short pause] [short pause] don't mourn. [short pause] Don't grieve. [short pause] This is a sacred day. [short pause] "The joy of the Lord is your strength."

Not happiness. [short pause] Happiness depends on what happens. [short pause] Joy is something deeper. [short pause] It's the kind that comes from knowing you belong to something unshakable, even when everything around you shakes. [short pause] It's the quiet confidence that you are known, you are held, and today has purpose... [short pause] [short pause] even if you can't see all of it yet.
So here's the question this morning: where are you trying to manufacture your own strength?

Where are you white-knuckling your way through... [short pause] [short pause] running on discipline and caffeine and sheer stubbornness... [short pause] [short pause] when what you actually need is to let joy back in?

Maybe it's a relationship that's draining you. [short pause] Maybe it's a project that feels impossible. [short pause] Maybe it's the general weight of responsibility that never seems to lighten. [short pause] Where are you carrying something that was never meant to be carried alone?

This is the invitation: stop manufacturing. [short pause] Start receiving. [short pause] Not passivity... [short pause] [short pause] partnership. [short pause] Your effort matters. [short pause] But it was never meant to be the foundation. [short pause] Joy is.

Lord... [short pause] [short pause] I confess that I try to be my own source most days. [short pause] I reach for control before I reach for you. [short pause] I mistake exhaustion for dedication, and stubbornness for strength. [short pause] Today, I want something different. [short pause] I want to draw from your joy instead of my reserves. [short pause] Teach me the difference between striving and trusting. [short pause] And when I forget... [short pause] [short pause] which I will... [short pause] [short pause] bring me back to this: the joy of the Lord is my strength. [short pause] Not mine. [short pause] Yours. [short pause] Amen.
Now see your day ahead. [short pause] Not the anxious version... [short pause] [short pause] the real one. [short pause] The version where you show up grounded instead of frantic.

See yourself walking into the first challenge with something steady behind your eyes. [short pause] Not because you have all the answers... [short pause] [short pause] but because you know where your strength actually comes from.

See yourself in the hard moment... [short pause] [short pause] the one you're already dreading a little... [short pause] [short pause] and instead of bracing, you breathe. [short pause] You remember. [short pause] Joy. [short pause] Not a feeling you have to produce. [short pause] A gift you have to receive.
Now see yourself at the end of the day. [short pause] Tired, maybe. [short pause] But not depleted. [short pause] Because you drew from a source that doesn't run dry.
Three things to carry with you. [short pause] Say them with me... [short pause] [short pause] out loud if you can, silently if you must.
"Today I will be present, not perfect."
"The joy of the Lord is my strength."
The day is before you, and the Lord goes with you. [short pause] His joy is not somewhere you have to travel to find... [short pause] [short pause] it's already here, already given, already enough.
Go in that strength. [short pause] And let his joy carry what your willpower cannot.
